title: Integrate tiktoken or equivalent for accurate per-section token counting

Persist vim.notify output in a toggleable floating window
description: All vim.notify calls from the tiktoken extension should be captured and persisted in a floating window. The window should be togglable via a keymap (default: <leader>tt or configurable). Entries should be timestamped and show log level. The window should auto-scroll to the latest entry. Implement as a notify_history module within the extension.
close_reason: Implemented notify_history module (notify_history.lua) with ring-buffer storage, toggleable floating window, timestamps, level highlights, auto-scroll, keymap support. Wired into init.lua: all nh.notify() calls replace vim.notify(); exports toggle_history_window, clear_history, get_history added.

title: List and evaluate codecompanion.nvim events for token counting suitability
Add estimated_tokens count to result
Lua error: attempt to index local 'speed' (a number value) in stat_line
description:
Error detected while processing User Autocommands for 'CodeCompanionChatDone':

Error executing lua callback: ...tiktoken/lua/codecompanion/_extensions/tiktoken/init.lua:165: attempt to index local 'speed' (a number value)

stack traceback:
  ...tiktoken/lua/codecompanion/_extensions/tiktoken/init.lua:165: in function 'stat_line'
  ...tiktoken/lua/codecompanion/_extensions/tiktoken/init.lua:353: in function <...tiktoken/lua/codecompanion/_extensions/tiktoken/init.lua:329>
  [C]: in function 'nvim_exec_autocmds'
  ...lazy/codecompanion.nvim/lua/codecompanion/utils/init.lua:10: in function 'fire'
  ...panion.nvim/lua/codecompanion/interactions/chat/init.lua:1360: in function 'done'
  ...panion.nvim/lua/codecompanion/interactions/chat/init.lua:1152: in function 'on_done'
  .../nvim/lazy/codecompanion.nvim/lua/codecompanion/http.lua:138: in function <.../nvim/lazy/codecompanion.nvim/lua/codecompanion/http.lua:134>

This occurs when processing the 'CodeCompanionChatDone' autocmd. The variable 'speed' is a number, but is being indexed as if it were a table. Fix the bug in stat_line to handle 'speed' correctly.

title: Add vim.notify toggle switch with gtt keymap
description: Add a module-level boolean flag 'notify_enabled' (default true) that gates every vim.notify call in the extension. Register a buffer-local (or global) keymap 'gtt' that toggles the flag and echoes the new state. The flag should be respected by ALL notify sites: ChatOpened, ChatSubmitted, ChatDone, ChatStopped, InlineFinished, ToolsFinished, ToolApprovalRequested. Optionally expose the toggle as an exported function so callers can integrate it into their own keymaps.
close_reason: Implemented notify_enabled toggle in init.lua: gtt keymap + opts.notify init flag + Extension.exports.toggle_notify. All 7 vim.notify sites gated. stylua and cargo test pass.

title: Implement actionable token intelligence UI: core metrics, deltas, and context breakdown
Refactor context assembly to tag and count each section (system, chat, tools, files, user)
Investigate and fix estimated_tokens discrepancy vs tiktoken count
description:
codecompanion.nvim uses a heuristic Lua function (tokens.calculate) to set _meta.estimated_tokens. This is a character-run-based approximation, NOT tiktoken BPE. The discrepancy is structural and expected, but needs to be understood and ideally surfaced clearly to users. See investigation notes below.

ROOT CAUSE:
tokens.calculate() in codecompanion.nvim/lua/codecompanion/utils/tokens.lua uses a heuristic:
- Alpha/alphanumeric runs: ceil(len / 6) tokens (default)
- Numeric-only runs: always 1 token
- Punctuation runs: ceil(len / 2) tokens
- Whitespace: 0 tokens
- Non-ASCII multi-byte sequences: ceil(bytes / 3) tokens

This is a fast approximation inspired by tokenx, not BPE.

Tiktoken (this project) does real BPE tokenisation - it accounts for sub-word splits, special tokens, and the actual model vocabulary.

KEY DISCREPANCIES:
1. Alphanumeric: heuristic uses fixed divisor (6), BPE varies by word/context.
2. Punctuation: heuristic treats all punctuation identically (ceil/2), BPE splits differently per model.
3. Multi-byte (CJK, emoji, etc.): heuristic uses ceil(bytes/3), BPE can assign very different token counts.
4. Overhead: heuristic adds 0 message overhead by default (message_overhead=0 in DEFAULTS). Tiktoken adds 3 tokens/message + role tokens + 3 priming.
5. Code/markup: code snippets and XML tags tokenise very differently between heuristic and BPE.

WHAT estimated_tokens IS USED FOR (in our extension):
- Displayed in stat_line as '(est: N)' alongside the tiktoken count - for comparison only.
- Summed across messages in count_messages result.estimated_tokens.
- NOT used to drive any logic in our extension.

SUGGESTED ACTION:
- Consider removing the est display or labelling it more clearly as 'heuristic'.
- Optionally, compute the ratio (tiktoken / estimated) and log it for calibration.
close_reason: Investigated root cause: estimated_tokens is a per-message heuristic sum from codecompanion.nvim, not BPE. Renamed to total_estimated and updated display label to 'heuristic total' for clarity. Committed and pushed.

title: count_messages panics with missing field _meta on CodeCompanionChatOpened
Replace per-tool notifications with aggregate CodeCompanionToolsFinished summary
description:
Currently CodeCompanionToolStarted/ToolFinished fires a notification for every individual tool call. When tools are frequent (e.g. agentic loops), this creates notification spam.

Instead:
- Remove per-tool ToolStarted/ToolFinished notifications
- Hook CodeCompanionToolsStarted to snapshot token count at the start of the tool cycle
- Hook CodeCompanionToolsFinished to show a single aggregate summary: total token delta across all tools in the cycle, elapsed time, how many tools ran

title: Remove floating window from token intelligence UI
description: Use vim.notify only for token metrics display. Simplify the UI.

title: Track generation tokens/s from model response
description: Hook into CodeCompanionChatSubmitted and CodeCompanionChatDone events to measure generation throughput. At Submitted, snapshot the current token count. At Done, call count_messages again and compute the delta (new_tokens - prompt_tokens). Divide by the wall-clock elapsed time between the two events to get generation tokens/s. Display this as a second llama.cpp-style line alongside the existing prompt-processing line, e.g.: ≈ 966 tokens  ⊙ 10s  ↺ 90.49 t/s (generation)

title: Investigate PR #2872 and integrate new callback into tiktoken extension
description:
Read https://github.com/olimorris/codecompanion.nvim/pull/2872, summarize the introduced callback (name, event timing, payload), and propose/implement how to leverage it in our codecompanion-tiktoken extension. Tasks:
1) Fetch and summarise PR #2872 (diff + rationale).
2) Identify the new callback's API and where it should be used in the tiktoken extension (replace or augment existing User autocmds).
3) Implement integration: subscribe to the callback, add tests, and update docs.
4) Run tests and lint, and prepare PR against our repo.
Acceptance criteria:
- A short summary of PR #2872 included in the issue description.
- Implementation plan with file paths and code sketch.
- Working patch or branch with tests.
Reference: https://github.com/olimorris/codecompanion.nvim/pull/2872
close_reason: Implemented on_tool_output integration: truncate tool outputs; added toggle_notify; added .luacheckrc; ran tests and lint; committed and pushed.

title: Check all events in codecompanion.nvim usage/events.md for suitability for token counting and aggregation (e.g., tokens/s read/write)
description:
Analysis of events for token counting and aggregation:

Events suitable for token counting and aggregation (e.g., tokens/s read/write):
- CodeCompanionRequestStarted
- CodeCompanionRequestStreaming
- CodeCompanionRequestFinished
- CodeCompanionChatSubmitted
- CodeCompanionChatDone
- CodeCompanionInlineStarted
- CodeCompanionInlineFinished

Reasoning: These events correspond to API calls, chat submissions, and inline interactions where tokens are read/written. They are ideal for counting tokens and measuring throughput. Other events are not directly related to token usage.

Recommended aggregates:
- Total tokens read/written per request
- Tokens per second during streaming
- Tokens per chat
- Tokens per inline interaction
- Aggregates by adapter/model (from event payloads)

Other events are not recommended for token counting.

title: Improve Lua event handling: add Stopped/Closed cleanup, use RequestStarted for timing
description: Add CodeCompanionChatStopped and CodeCompanionChatClosed handlers to clean up submit_snapshots. Use CodeCompanionRequestStarted instead of ChatSubmitted for more accurate wall-clock timing of LLM generation.
